item.title: S01E02
item.description: Urban Flooding and Digital Models: A Conversation with Franzi From civil engineering student to urban flood researcher, Franzi Tügel demonstrates how water moves through cities in unexpected ways.

item.title: S01E03
item.description: Flash Droughts and Community Voices: A Conversation with Pedro From drought-prone Brazil to water-rich Germany, eco-hydrologist Pedro Alencar studies flash droughts through an unusual dual approach.

item.title: S01E04
item.description: Water Governance in Multiple Voices: A Conversation with Kei Water governance researcher Kei Namba brings a unique cross-cultural perspective, contrasting Japan’s disaster-driven risk awareness with Germany’s approach to water management.

item.title: S01E01
item.description: Introducing Touching Water We started this podcast because we are interested in exploring how to approach water.

item.title: index
item.description: Touching Water: a Science Podcast Welcome to the Touching Water Podcast portal! Here you can find all our episodes, shownotes and extra information about our podcast.
